summary: Backend integration testing of ZeroMark AI complete. 37/37 tests pass covering Auth, Business, Leads, Campaigns, Approvals, AI generation (Groq), Dashboard, Reports, Scraping (AI-generated leads), Subscription/Razorpay.

minor.issue: Password min_length=6 is weak (industry standard is 8+); also register endpoint doesn't enforce password complexity.
Falls back to cookie when no Authorization header. Works correctly but means a logged-in browser session can hit endpoints without explicit token; expected for cookie auth but worth documenting.
No brute-force lockout / rate limiting per playbook (5-fail lockout). Currently unlimited login attempts allowed.

Cookie missing 'secure=True' and not 'samesite=none' which is required for cross-site (frontend on different domain than backend) - cookie may not be sent for cross-origin requests in production. Bearer token works fine; cookie auth from browser may break.
CORS_ORIGINS is set to '*' in .env while allow_credentials=True. Browsers reject wildcard origin with credentials; should be explicit origin list.
No upper bound on bulk size; could be abused for huge inserts.
Always pulls all leads (limit 500), no segmentation/targeting. Synchronous SMTP/Twilio loop will timeout for large lists; should be async/background.

action_items: Tighten CORS: replace wildcard with explicit allowed origins since allow_credentials=True is set (browsers will reject '*' + credentials).
Add cookie 'secure=True' and 'samesite=none' so cookie auth works across origins; or document that frontend must use Bearer token (currently does).
Add login brute-force lockout (per auth playbook: lock after 5 failed attempts).
Move /api/campaigns/{id}/send to a background task; current implementation blocks the request loop and will time out on >50 leads.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~977 lines - approaching split threshold; consider modularising (auth.py, leads.py, campaigns.py, ai.py, billing.py).
Approval modify endpoint allows ANY user-supplied content to overwrite campaign content with no length/sanitization checks.
Razorpay client initialised on every checkout/verify call - okay but could be cached.
smtplib in run_in_executor is synchronous-per-lead; for production use aiosmtplib or queue.
Trial days calc: datetime.fromisoformat may throw on bad data; wrap in try/except.
_ai_generate_sample_leads inserts straight into leads collection without dedup against existing email/phone.
Password min length of 6 chars is weak; recommend >=8 with at least one digit/symbol.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed leads, campaigns, business profile; session-scoped fixture cleans up TEST_ leads & campaigns at end. Reports/scraping jobs/approvals are not cleaned (no DELETE endpoints).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend endpoints from review_request verified end-to-end. Razorpay test mode creates real test orders; signature verify correctly rejects bad signatures. Groq AI generation (EMAIL/SMS) returns content within ~2-5s. Real SMTP/Twilio sends executed during test_send_campaign_after_approval - they may rate-limit but endpoint returns 200 with sent/failed counts. CORS '*' + credentials=True is the most likely production-time blocker for browser cookie auth.
rca of the issue: Two initial test failures were both test-side issues, not backend bugs: (1) backend lowercases emails on register but test asserted exact case; (2) shared requests.Session retained admin cookie from earlier login, so unauth-check returned 200 - fixed by using a fresh session. After fixes, 100% pass.
